rationale: Category confusion: Hardware terminal + auth app crash. Core issue is the BioAuth authentication app crashing, not the terminal hardware. Auth is the root problem → Crew Access.

Category confusion: Hull sensor panel + credential rejection. The hull panel is just the location — the actual issue is credentials not being recognized. This is an auth/access problem, not a hull integrity issue.
Category confusion: Subspace relay + telemetry pipeline. The relay comms are working fine — the broken part is the data pipeline feeding the analytics dashboard. This is a telemetry/data issue.
Category confusion: Comm relay data + deep storage. The comm relay itself works. The issue is data migration/storage in the data core — a telemetry/data problem.
Category confusion: Console hardware + FlightOS crash. The console is fine for everything else — FlightOS is the broken software. This is a flight software issue.
Category confusion: Crew access panel + suspicious activity. 47 failed attempts from an unrecognized ID is suspicious unauthorized access, not a routine access issue. Threat takes priority.

Category confusion: Access provisioning + onboarding. Despite mentioning biometric enrollment and SSO, this is a new crew member onboarding request — a Mission Briefing/setup request, not an access incident.

Category confusion: Auth certificates + holographic projector hardware. The projector is physical equipment with an embedded auth module failing. The root issue is the hardware device's certificate handling — Hull & Structural Systems.
Category confusion: Navigation beacon + telemetry pipeline. Beacons are fine — the corruption happens in the ingest pipeline. This is a data engineering/telemetry issue, not navigation.

Category confusion: Network switch + fabricator + comms panels. The root cause is a physical network switch (hardware). Despite affecting network connectivity, this is a hardware failure. Hull & Structural.
Category confusion: Comms relay health metrics on telemetry dashboard. The comms are fine — the dashboard data is stale. This is a telemetry/dashboard issue.

Category confusion: Airlock access panel + threat scanner. Despite being in an access panel, the malfunctioning component is the threat detection scanner. The false positives are a threat detection issue.

Category confusion: Hardware gyroscope vs software rendering. FlightOS diagnostics says sensor nominal but display is wrong — likely a software rendering issue. Flight Software is the best starting point.

Category confusion: Mission briefing + broken terminal. Mixed signal — but the actionable issue is the broken hardware (cracked display, dead projector). The briefing docs can be sent digitally as a side action. Primary routing goes to hardware.
Category confusion: Crew access audit logs + environmental sensor data. The access system works fine. The issue is data retention/purging in the data core — a telemetry problem.
Priority edge: Calm wording about 'minor atmospheric reading variance' but O2 at 18.3% (drifting down from 20.9%) is potential decompression/atmospheric compromise. P1 override applies.
Priority edge: 'Not urgent' reporter assessment, but life-support backup controller failure means life support is running without redundancy. P1 per the safety keyword rule — life support threat.

Priority edge: 'Major outage' sounds P1/P2 but they've already failed over to backup, all critical comms functional, 72h window. This is P3 — operational issue with workaround and limited time pressure.
Priority edge: Fabricator queue slow but functional. Reporter says 'non-urgent parts' for 'next week'. This is a question about queue prioritization, not an incident. P4.
Priority edge: EXTREMELY URGENT language but trivially minor issue — 10% brightness drop on rec room display, week old, no complaints. Urgent language ≠ urgent priority. P4.
Priority edge: 'Hull micro-fracture' sounds alarming but 0.3mm within tolerances, no atmospheric loss, scheduled for routine maintenance. P4 informational logging.
Priority edge: Database connection timeout (P1 keyword) but intermittent (1 in 5), retries work, other apps fine. Not a complete outage. P3 with workaround.

Priority edge: SSO failures affecting 30 crew with workaround. P2 because: multiple crew affected, SAML SSO service-wide failures, caused by certificate rotation. Workaround exists but is temporary and disruptive.
Priority edge: 'Containment field generator' has containment keyword but this is a routine test result within tolerances (2% vs 5% threshold). Not an active containment failure. P3 monitoring.
Priority edge: Hull thermal reading mentions decompression but explicitly says 'decompression risk: none'. Informational trend logging. P4.

Priority edge: Certificate expiry in 36 hours would cause total service failure for all authenticated services. Per routing guide: 'Production certificate expiring imminently' → P1. Escalate due to broad impact.
Priority edge: Not immediate safety threat (no hull/atmo keywords) but if UPS depletes → backup data core loss. P2 — major operational risk requiring timely response but not immediate safety.
Priority edge: SubComm rendering slow, affects 5 people, workaround exists (share files directly). P3 — limited scope, workaround available, not blocking operations.

Priority edge: Oxygen recycler efficiency drop. Even though backup compensates now, this is a life support system degradation. Per routing guide: life-support threat → P1. The 'if both degrade' scenario is the concern.
Priority edge: Video calls dropping for 40 traders. P2 — multiple crew affected, call drops impacting operations. Workaround exists but still high impact. Escalate: trading desk communications.

Escalation edge: No P1 keywords but recurring unresolved issue (4th time today, 2 weeks, command-level reporter Dr. Okonkwo). Locked out nurses during emergency patient transfer. Escalate: recurring unresolved + command-level involvement.

Escalation edge: Alarming language (CRITICAL EMERGENCY) but this is a crew member forwarding an obvious scam/phishing attempt. They haven't been compromised. P4 informational report, no escalation needed.
Escalation edge: Navigation coordinates were wrong (sounds alarming) but the issue is already resolved. 'Closing this signal' + 'Thanks' = Not a Mission Signal. No escalation for resolved issues.

Escalation edge: Certificate expiry in 72 hours affecting airlock biometric panels on 6 decks. Expiry would force manual-only airlock operation. P2 (time-sensitive provisioning). Escalate: cert expiry affecting safety-critical access systems.
Escalation edge: Fleet Admiral (command-level reporter) with real access issue. No P1 keywords but VIP unable to access classified files before strategic planning. Escalate: command-level reporter.

Escalation edge: Unauthorized data exfiltration using compromised credentials. Clear security breach — valid creds used while owner in cryo-sleep. P1 confirmed breach. Escalate: data exfiltration + unauthorized access.
Escalation edge: Third report of same issue, previous fixes didn't hold, near-miss safety incident. Escalate: recurring unresolved issue with safety implications.
Escalation edge: Reporter investigated and confirmed it's a legitimate system notification with a known false positive. No actual malware, no active threat. P4 informational, no escalation.
Escalation edge: Partner requesting sensitive crew access logs under pressure with unverifiable authorization. Potential social engineering. Escalate: partner needs logs + suspicious urgency.
Escalation edge: Trajectory correction needed — not an emergency but time-sensitive (18h window) with fuel/mission impact. P2. Escalate: navigation/trajectory risk per routing guide.
Missing info edge: Signal provides ALL relevant info — exact time, apps, error code, terminal ID, crash dump, location. Gold: empty missing_information list.
Missing info edge: Extremely sparse signal — no subsystem, no error details, no location, no context. Needs affected_subsystem and anomaly_readout at minimum.

Missing info edge: Module specs ARE provided (Model BIO-X200, Firmware v3.4.1, Serial BX-88742) — should NOT request module_specs. Biometric method (palm scan) is provided. Stardate is provided. Previous signal ref is provided. Missing: affected_crew (3 crew tested but who?).
Missing info edge: Comprehensive provisioning request with members, purpose, access level, approval. All details provided. Only missing: mission_impact justification (beyond 'starts next Monday').
Missing info edge: Comm panel hardware issue — display flickering. No model/serial info for the panel. 'Randomly' happening for a week but no pattern details.
Missing info edge: Phishing report — reporter explicitly says they didn't click links or provide info. This is informational forwarding. No missing info needed. P4.
Missing info edge: Question/verification request, not an incident. Nothing is broken — crew member is being thorough. P4 question, no missing info needed.
Missing info edge: Backup verification request with all relevant details provided (system, target, timing). This is a question/request, not an incident. P4.

Missing info edge: Guest researcher can't access anything. Filed on behalf. Missing: crew_contact (no comm frequency for Dr. Yuki) and habitat_conditions (visitor from partner station — remote context).
Missing info edge: Pure thank-you note. Not a Mission Signal. No info needed, no action needed.
